title: Fuel and emission control structure description - On-board fault detection device

blocks.text: Fuel and emission control structure description - On-board fault detection device

The on-board failure detection system detects failures of the emission control system and warns the driver with the PGM-FI warning light in the combination meter when a failure is detected in any of the failure detection items, thereby alerting the driver of the failure. When a misfire is detected, the PGM-FI warning light blinks depending on the condition, and lights up when other malfunctions are detected. Failure detection items are Failure diagnosis table by DTC As shown in the following table.
The failure detection function of some systems and sensors uses a two-consecutive failure detection method.

When an abnormality occurs, the PGM-FI ECU stores the condition at that time as a temporary abnormality, and after the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode → ON mode, the PGM-FI warning light is turned on when the abnormality occurs again in the same condition. The information stored by the PGM-FI ECU as a temporary abnormality can be checked by the HDS (Honda Diagnostic System) as a temporary DTC, but if it is determined to be transient under certain conditions, the stored information is automatically deleted.

If the SCS terminal of the data link coupler (16P) is shorted, this function is deactivated and the PGM-FI warning light immediately blinks when an error is detected. This facilitates failure diagnosis.

This vehicle is compatible with Honda's diagnostic system, HDS. HDS can be used by connecting a general-purpose PC with HDS installed via MVCI to the data link coupler (16P) located under the left foot of the driver's seat.
The data link coupler has the following terminals for communication with HDS and power supply to the MVCI

rows.text: HDS communication signal terminal (models equipped with Honda Smart Key System)
Ground terminal for HDS communication signal
F-CAN communication terminal (for PGM-FI system communication)
Terminals for communication with systems except PGM-FI system
F-CAN communication terminal (for PGM-FI system communication)
Terminal for communication with SRS system
